items.title: 'This is a special club' | Andoni relishing Liverpool challenge ahead
items.summary: Andoni Iraola is excited by the challenge ahead after being confirmed as the new Liverpool manager. Watch the full interview for free on All Red Video

items.title: Celtic fan groups sign letter opposing Keane appointment
items.summary: Dozens of Celtic supporters clubs have put their names to a statement opposing the potential appointment of Robbie Keane as manager over his previous role with Maccabi Tel Aviv.

items.title: Spieth sinks UNBELIEVABLE 63ft putt facing completely the wrong way!
items.summary: Jordan Spieth holed a 63-foot putt, facing completely the wrong way on the sixth hole of the Memorial Tournament in Dublin, Ohio.

items.title: Alphonsi disappointed by Smith punishment over sexist social media post
items.summary: Former England international Maggie Alphonsi has criticised the punishment given to Rugby Football Union council member Matthew Smith for posting discriminatory criticism of her.

items.title: Celtic fan groups state opposition to Keane
items.summary: Several Celtic supporters clubs put their names to a statement opposing the potential appointment of Robbie Keane as manager because of the Irishman's previous role as Maccabi Tel Aviv head coach.

items.title: NI's record side show grit beyond their years
items.summary: Northern Ireland, fielding a record youthful side, showed resilience beyond their years in a 1-0 friendly win over Guinea in Spain.

items.title: Papers: Arsenal receive huge boost in race for Kroupi Jr
items.summary: The top stories and transfer rumours from Friday's newspapers...

items.title: Liverpool step up Diomande pursuit - Friday's gossip
items.summary: Liverpool make contact with RB Leipzig over a move for Yan Diomande, Arsenal face competition from PSG for two players, Cologne winger Said El Mala not keen on Brentford move, plus more.

items.title: Liverpool transfer target Alex Scott tells fans to ‘get excited’ with ‘aggressive’ Iraola to bring return of heavy metal
items.summary: ALEX SCOTT predicts that heavy metal football will be returning to Anfield - while he could be on the way there himself. The Bournemouth midfielder is adamant that incoming Liverpool manager Andoni Iraola, who spent three years on the south coast, will deliver the same edge-of-your-seat, high-intens

items.title: Liverpool name Iraola head coach, replacing Slot
items.summary: Liverpool have confirmed the appointment of Andoni Iraola as their new head coach.

items.title: City warn Madrid with legal action over Haaland
items.summary: Manchester City have threatened legal action after Real Madrid presidential candidate Enrique Riquelme promised to sign Erling Haaland if he's elected.

items.title: England stars urged to avoid taking SLEEPING PILLS as they get to grips with toughest-ever World Cup in North America
items.summary: ENGLAND stars have been warned to avoid taking SLEEPING PILLS as they get to grips with the toughest-ever World Cup. Thomas Tuchel’s squad have spent the last four days training at Palm Beach Gardens ahead of tomorrow’s warm-up friendly against New Zealand in Tampa. The Three Lions face Costa Rica i

items.title: Only England could possibly turn Jude Bellingham into a dilemma but Thomas Tuchel has made a statement: Go and deliver
items.summary: IN no other country would there be a debate around the best player and whether they should be a starter at the World Cup. It’s a uniquely English situation we find ourselves in with Jude Bellingham, just six days before the start of the tournament. Thomas Tuchel has been lukewarm on him from the mom

items.title: Jose Mourinho’s shock return to Real Madrid set to cost LaLiga giants monster fee as Benfica release statement
items.summary: REAL MADRID will have to pay Benfica up to £13MILLION in compensation if they appoint Jose Mourinho. The Spanish giants are expected to announce the return of Mourinho in the coming days - if president Florentino Perez keeps his job. Perez has promised to bring the Special One back to the Bernabeu i

items.title: CEO: Man Utd will follow PL template for signings
items.summary: Manchester United CEO Omar Berrada has hinted that the club will again look to target players with Premier League experience in the transfer window after signing Bryan Mbeumo and Matheus Cunha from domestic rivals last summer.

items.title: Yamal-less Spain held by Iraq in WC sendoff
items.summary: Spain were left frustrated in their first World Cup warm-up match as they laboured to a 1-1 draw with Iraq at the Estadio de Riazor in La Coruña.

items.title: Japan ditches pre-WC training site over poor pitch...
items.summary: Japan has changed practice venues in Monterrey ahead of the World Cup after players encountered an uneven pitch marred by patches of dirt at their original training ground.

items.title: Benfica: Madrid must pay $17M to sign Mourinho
items.summary: Real Madrid will have to pay Benfica €15 million to sign José Mourinho as coach if Florentino Pérez is re-elected as Madrid president, the Portuguese club said on Thursday.

items.title: FIFA ban water bottles inside stadiums at WC
items.summary: FIFA has made a late change of policy to bar World Cup fans from bringing refillable water bottles into the tournament's 16 stadiums across North America.

items.title: Giuliani: WC security challenge 'unprecedented'
items.summary: This summer's World Cup will pose an unprecedented security challenge due to its size and scope, but the nation's law enforcement is "leaning in," Andrew Giuliani, executive director of the White House FIFA World Cup 2026 Task Force, told ESPN.

items.title: 📈 Ranking all 48 World Cup squads
items.summary: We have the final 26-player squads that can win the FIFA World Cup, so let's go through all 48 teams and rank where they stand.
